title: Duplicated ArchivalMemory row→struct decode across 3 sites + 5 other entity types
description:
## Severity
Minor
## Dimension
dry
## Location
src/memory.rs:412-424 (memory_search), 447-461 (memory_get), 519-532 (memory_list); plus identical pattern for RecentSession (734-754), RecentActivity (802-812), CodingPattern (968-980), ErrorPattern (1091-1102), LearnedPreference (1181-1192).
## Violation
The exact same six-field decode closure is duplicated three times for `ArchivalMemory`:

```rust
Ok(ArchivalMemory {
    id: row.get(0)?,
    content: row.get(1)?,
    tags: row.get::<_, String>(2)?.split(',').filter(|s| !s.is_empty()).map(String::from).collect(),
    created_at: row.get(3)?,
    updated_at: row.get(4)?,
})
```

If `archival_memory` ever gets a new column or the tag-storage scheme changes (see #464), three call sites must change in lockstep. The same anti-pattern repeats for every entity type — five more × 1-3 sites each. Total: ~12 duplicated decode blocks.
## Mandated refactor
1. Implement a free function `archival_memory_from_row(row: &Row) -> rusqlite::Result<ArchivalMemory>` and call it from all three query_map closures. Same for each other entity type — one helper each.
2. Or introduce a `TryFrom<&Row>` trait impl per type (more idiomatic Rust).
3. The tag-split logic is itself duplicated 3x — extract `fn parse_tags(s: &str) -> Vec<String>`.
4. After consolidation, the column-ordering knowledge lives in exactly one place per type, satisfying SSOT.
## Filed from QA review dispatch
